comprehension_detail.note: Backwards law fully quoted (ch1§2¶20): Alan Watts' idea that pursuing positive experience is itself negative because pursuit reinforces lack; applied to happiness via 'the more you desperately want to be happy and loved, the lonelier you become.' Inverse stated in ch1§2¶29.

Feedback Loop from Hell defined (ch1§2¶5) with the verbatim 'I'm such a loser for doing it' self-judgment example; the ch1 SVG diagram externalizes the spiral (anxiety → anxiety about anxiety → …). Perpetuated by consumer culture / social media breeding the belief that negative experiences are not okay (ch1, body prose).

ch2§3¶7 states people 'fuck things up in at least one of two ways' and ch2§3¶3 names the two failure modes (avoiding problems / feeling you have none, OR feeling you have problems you can't solve). The two preventions are present in substance but the list is introduced ('two ways') without both bullets enumerated crisply in one place — a reader must stitch ch2§3¶3 together. Answerable but slightly fragmented.

ch3 SVG diagram is explicit: face one = 'I'm awesome, everyone else sucks', face two = 'I suck, everyone else is awesome', labeled 'two flavors, same disease' → ENTITLEMENT. Shared belief = needing to feel good about oneself all the time / feeling special or uniquely defective (ch3§1¶16, ch3§2¶44).

Self-Awareness Onion diagram + ch4§2¶16: Layer 1 what we feel, Layer 2 why we feel it, Layer 3 = personal values/metrics. Third is most important because 'our values determine the nature of our problems, and the nature of our problems determines the quality of our lives' (ch4§2¶17).

ch4§5¶1-3: good values are reality-based, socially constructive, immediate/controllable. Honesty = good value (controllable, reality-based, benefits others); popularity = bad value (out of control, not reality-based). Pleasure/material success/always-being-right/staying-positive given as bad-value examples (ch4§4).

ch5§3¶15-20 + responsibility≠fault SVG: fault is past tense (blame, fixed), responsibility is present tense (your response, chosen). Baby-on-doorstep (ch5§3¶17) and robbery (ch5§4¶2) examples show you can be responsible for managing what is not your fault.

Manson's Law of Avoidance verbatim (ch6§5¶4): 'The more something threatens your identity, the more you will avoid it.' Narrow identity = fragile because 'the narrower and rarer the identity you choose, the more everything will seem to threaten you' (ch6§6¶10); define yourself in ordinary ways.

Do Something principle: ch7§4¶8 'Action isn't just the effect of motivation; it's also the cause of it', explicit reordering ch7§4¶15 'Action → Inspiration → Motivation', reversing the conventional inspiration→motivation→action chain (also shown in ch7 SVG). ch7§4¶16 instructs: do anything, harness the reaction.

ch9§2¶15-20: Becker's immortality projects = conceptual self built to outlive the physical self (names on buildings, children, art). 'Our immortality projects are our values' (ch9§2¶19). Bitter antidote (ch9§2¶20) = questioning the conceptual self and becoming comfortable with one's own death rather than pursuing immortality. Becker's-two-deaths SVG reinforces it.

verdict.visual_pleasure: This reads like a proper Distill/Gwern-grade document, not a content dump. Calm serif body (Iowan/Source Serif) at a disciplined ~66ch measure, generous 1.65 leading, an accent-orange drop cap opening each chapter, accent-ruled anchor blockquotes, and full-width italic pull-quotes that give the eye a rhythm break. Dark-mode is wired via prefers-color-scheme. The thing I'd keep scrolling for is the cadence: opener image → drop cap → source-anchored quote → concept diagram, repeated nine times, so the page never feels like a wall. It falls short of a 5 because the prose layer is overwhelmingly verbatim blockquotes — long stretches are quote-after-quote with only thin connective sentences, which visually reads as a stack of bordered boxes rather than flowing editorial text. That box-stacking is handsome but slightly monotonous over 62 minutes. Solid 4.
verdict.navigation: The sticky left-rail TOC with all nine chapters, scroll-spy via IntersectionObserver that lights the current chapter, a top progress bar plus a live '% read' and per-chapter reading-time chips ('~7 min') give me real orientation — I always know where I am and how much is left. The linear design-note rationale is honest and correct for a cumulative argument. Why not 5: the TOC stops at chapter level; with nine long chapters each containing 5-7 H3 subsections (Disappointment Panda, Shitty Values, Manson's Law, etc.), there are no sub-anchors to jump to a section, and the cross-reference (.xref) hover-preview chrome described in the contract isn't actually used anywhere in the body — every internal link just points back to the chapter opener. Navigation is frictionless top-level but shallow within chapters. 4.
verdict.comprehension: This is the standout dimension. I answered 9.5 of 10 test questions from the artifact alone, and the half-miss (Q3's 'two ways') is a sequencing nit, not a content gap. Every framework that matters — backwards law, Feedback Loop from Hell, happiness-as-problem-solving, entitlement's two faces, the self-awareness onion, good vs bad values with criteria and examples, responsibility≠fault, Manson's Law of Avoidance, the Do Something principle, Becker's immortality projects and bitter antidote — is present in Manson's own words with source-span IDs (chN§x¶y) on every quote, which is unusually trustworthy. I could pass a quiz on this book having never touched the original. Coverage is footnoted at 29.7% of source, but the 29.7% chosen is the load-bearing 29.7%: the spine of the argument survives intact. 5.
verdict.illustration: Per instruction I treat the nine {{CB:chN}} tokens as present hand-drawn chapter openers (note: in this pre-assembly design.html they appear as literal tokens, not yet base64-embedded — they resolve in the shipped file). The real work is done by the nine inline-SVG Channel A concept diagrams, and these genuinely externalize relationships rather than decorate: the Feedback Loop as a four-node cycle, the problem→action→solution→new-problem engine, the concentric self-awareness onion, the fault-vs-responsibility split down a dashed divider, certainty-vs-doubt, the reordered Action→Inspiration→Motivation loop, breadth-vs-depth, and Becker's two deaths feeding the immortality project. For a voice-driven self-help book these diagrams are exactly the dual-coding that aids recall, and each opener has a companion diagram (anti-decoration rule honored). Held to 4 not 5 because several diagrams are fairly literal restatements of a sentence (e.g., the ch3 entitlement two-box, the ch8 breadth/depth arrow) rather than revealing structure the prose didn't already make obvious, and a couple have minor label cramping. Genuinely useful, not quite indispensable.
verdict.books_finished_rate: Terminal question, answered honestly: yes, this measurably increases my books-finished rate. The original is a ~200-page, profanity-paced trade paperback that takes me several hours; this artifact delivers the complete argumentative spine — every named framework, in Manson's own voice, source-anchored — in a 62-minute scroll I can finish in one sitting, with diagrams that lock the concepts in. After reading only this I can confidently discuss the backwards law, values/metrics, responsibility≠fault, and Becker without bluffing. That is the whole proposition of a condensed reader and it lands. It is a 4, not a 5, because what I'd skip in the original is also what makes Manson Manson: the long anecdotal set-pieces (Onoda, Mustaine, the Erin story, the cliff-jump) are compressed to one-line bridges, so I get the skeleton and the thesis but lose the narrative texture and some of the persuasive force that makes the ideas stick emotionally. I'd finish more books — I just wouldn't claim I'd *felt* this one the way the original makes you feel it. Clears the terminal bar.
verdict._image_note: design.html contains the 9 {{CB:chN}} tokens unrendered (this is the Designer-stage file); the prompt confirms all 9 are base64-embedded in the shipped/assembled artifact. Scored as present per instruction.
